question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `stateFilterCheck()` is called on every opcode execution (not just at call boundaries) to cause O(N) filter evaluations where N is the total opcode count across all concurrent requests, increasing CPU consumption by over 30% compared to baseline?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `contractFilterCheck()` performs a `HashSet.contains()` lookup on every opcode execution, and by submitting many concurrent requests to contracts not in the set, cause sustained HashSet lookup overhead that increases CPU consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: tracePostExecution() via TraceProperties] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract call that triggers a REVERT with a maximally large revert reason (up to the gas limit), causing `frame.getRevertReason().orElse(Bytes.EMPTY).toHexString()` to allocate a large string in `log.info()`, and by repeating this across many requests, increase node memory and I/O resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a contract that uses CREATE2 to deploy sub-contracts in a loop, where each deployment triggers many opcodes all passing `stateFilterCheck()` (empty `status` set), causing a multiplicative log emission rate that increases node resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user deploy a contract that makes recursive DELEGATECALL operations to itself, where each recursive call frame passes `contractFilterCheck()` (contract not in filter set), causing O(depth) log entries per transaction and increasing node resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: tracePostExecution() via TraceProperties] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract call with large calldata that is forwarded via CALLDATACOPY to sub-calls, causing `frame.getInputData().toShortHexString()` to produce large strings at every call depth level in `log.info()`, amplifying log I/O and string allocation overhead above the 30% resource consumption threshold?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `stateFilterCheck()` is evaluated before `contractFilterCheck()` in `tracePostExecution()`, and by ensuring the state always passes the filter, force the more expensive `commonEntityAccessor.get()` database lookup to execute on every opcode, increasing database I/O resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract call to a contract whose `recipientAddress` resolves to `Optional.empty()` from `commonEntityAccessor.get()`, causing `contractFilterCheck()` to be skipped (the `recipientNum.isPresent()` guard fails) and all opcodes to be logged unconditionally, increasing node resource consumption by over 30%?
